newsSince: No new material company-specific news since June 9. The string of U.S. data-center power wins (e.g. ~$70M big-tech order, ~$75M transformer deal, $300bn-won hyperscale project) were all disclosed April-May 2026 and are in the thesis. The +5.9% move since June 9 is a flow bounce within a drawdown from the May peak, not a new catalyst.

sources.label: LS Electric lands $70M US data-center power deal (Korea Herald)
LS Electric North America data-center power equipment project (TradingView/Reuters)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha lens: real data-center switchgear/transformer demand, but the order flow is April-May and already known; nothing new since June 9. At +136% YTD with ~18 buy ratings the name is no longer under-owned enough to re-rank up on a flow bounce. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Repeat US hyperscale power wins (switchgear, EHV transformers) tie it directly to the AI data-center power bottleneck.
verdicts.neutral: Farther from the pure passive/power stack; demand strong but well-discovered after +136% YTD.
verdicts.bear: Crowded, multiple buy ratings; no new catalyst since June 9 to restore asymmetry.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Additional US/Asia data-center power equipment orders
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding; project timing and competition in grid equipment

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent/under-covered power-&-grid lens: LS Electric is the Korean infra play on data-center power and grid automation with overseas-expansion optionality. The US order momentum supports the theme but is unchanged since June 9. Conviction medium; hold rank.
verdicts.bull: Korea/Asia data-center power + grid automation + overseas (US) expansion; less global coverage than US peers.
verdicts.neutral: Increasingly covered after repeated US wins; latent-discovery edge is narrowing.
verdicts.bear: Power & grid is becoming a consensus AI trade; +136% YTD reduces the under-followed premium.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Overseas data-center power expansion / new grid-automation wins
verdicts.primaryRisk: Loss of under-coverage premium; execution on overseas projects
